name: Mom's Cornbread Stuffing
recipeIngredient: 1/8 tsp Ground pepper, or to taste
1 tbsp Ground dry sage, or to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the cornmeal, baking soda, baking powder, salt, and flour.
2. In a separate bowl, mix together buttermilk and egg.
3. Pour the liquid ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir until combined; do not beat.
4. Heat salad oil in an oven-safe skillet or pan over very hot heat.
5. Bake the cornbread mixture in a preheated 450°F (232°C) oven for 10 to 12 minutes, then remove from the pan and let cool.
6. Cook celery and onion in a small amount of broth until tender; do not drain. Set aside.
7. Toast bread slices until crisp all the way through.
8. Crumble cornbread and toasted bread into a large bowl.
9. Add cooked celery, onion, and their cooking liquid to the bowl.
10. If desired, add chopped hard-boiled eggs.
11. Beat raw eggs and add them to the mixture.
12. Gradually add broth until the desired moisture level is reached; more broth will result in a wetter dressing.
13. Add seasonings and mix well.
14. Pour the mixture into a greased baking pan and bake at 350°F (175°C) for 35 to 45 minutes, or until nicely browned.
